intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Buda marina." However, based on the available data, I found the plant "Buddleja mariana" or "Buda marina" is an alternative name for "Buddleja mariana" commonly known as Marina Butterfly Bush.

The Buddleja mariana is a flowering plant species in the family Scrophulariaceae, native to the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Buddleja mariana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Buddleja mariana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
